name: Fremantle (Perth)
summary: Fremantle is the salty old port town that guards Perth, Western Australia's sunny capital city. Cruise ships tie up beside heritage stone buildings, and a quick ferry ride away is Rottnest Island, home to the smiley little quokka.
funFacts: Perth is Australia's sunniest capital city, with about 3,200 hours of sunshine and 139 totally clear days every year.
Rottnest Island is home to about 10,000 quokkas, fluffy hopping marsupials famous as 'the world's happiest-looking animal'.
Fremantle Prison was built by convicts in the 1850s using their own hands, and prisoners were locked inside until 1991.
The Swan River was named by Dutch sailors in 1697 who were amazed to spot black swans there, instead of white ones.
Kings Park sits high on a hill above Perth and is bigger than New York's Central Park, with treetop walkways for huge views.
thingsToDo: Take a 'Convict' or 'Escape' tour of Fremantle Prison and walk through the old stone cell blocks.
Catch the fast Rottnest Express ferry from Fremantle and try to snap a smiley quokka selfie.
Climb the historic Round House at Arthur Head and watch the One O'Clock Gun fire over the ocean.
Hop the Transperth train into Perth city and ride up Kings Park's treetop walkway over the gum trees.
Browse the buzzing Fremantle Markets for snacks, magic tricks and street performers in the heart of Freo.
Splash in the gentle waves and shady pine trees of famous Cottesloe Beach as the sun sets.

wildlife.name: Quokka
wildlife.description: Fluffy little hopping marsupial that looks like it's always smiling, found wild on Rottnest Island.

wildlife.name: Black Swan
wildlife.description: Beautiful glossy-black swan with a bright red beak, the symbol of Western Australia.

wildlife.name: Little Penguin
wildlife.description: World's smallest penguin, only 30cm tall, that waddles ashore at Penguin Island near Fremantle.

wildlife.name: Bottlenose Dolphin
wildlife.description: Friendly grey dolphin that swims and surfs in the waves along WA's coast.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Noongar, the language of the Whadjuk people of the Perth area.

images.alt: Perth's shiny city skyline rises above the Swan River at Elizabeth Quay.
Three big cruise ships docked side by side at Fremantle's busy harbour.
The tall stone gatehouse of historic Fremantle Prison under a bright blue sky.
The Round House, Fremantle's old stone prison from 1831, perched at Arthur Head.
Soft white sand and turquoise waves stretch along sunny Cottesloe Beach.
Perth city skyline seen across the Swan River from Kings Park's lookout.
A smiling little quokka peeks up close at the camera on Rottnest Island.
A glossy black swan with a bright red beak glides on still water.
A tiny Little Penguin waddles across the sand toward the sea.
A friendly bottlenose dolphin pokes its head above the water at Monkey Mia.
